title: Is Ceramic Coating Worth It? A Complete Guide
summary: Ceramic coating offers long-lasting protection and a deep gloss. Here's everything you need to know before investing in it for your vehicle.
content:
Ceramic coating has become one of the most popular protective treatments in auto detailing — and for good reason.

What is ceramic coating?

A liquid polymer that chemically bonds to your vehicle's factory paint, creating a durable, hydrophobic layer of protection.

The benefits

It repels water, dirt and UV rays, reduces the risk of swirl marks, and keeps your car looking glossy for years with minimal maintenance.

Is it worth it?

If you want long-term protection and a showroom shine without constant waxing, ceramic coating is absolutely worth the investment. Contact Albert Auto Detailing for a free quote.

title: How Often Should You Detail Your Car?
summary: Regular detailing protects your investment and keeps your car looking its best. Here's how often you should book a detail.
content:
Detailing isn't just about looks — it protects your paint, interior and resale value.

The general rule

For most drivers, a full detail every 3 to 4 months keeps your vehicle protected and looking great.

Factors that matter

Climate, parking conditions, mileage and whether you have a ceramic coating all affect how often you should detail.

Maintenance between details

Regular washes and interior wipe-downs extend the life of each detail. Book your next appointment with Albert Auto Detailing in Norwalk.

title: Paint Correction Explained: Restore Your Shine
summary: Swirl marks and scratches dulling your paint? Paint correction can restore a flawless, mirror-like finish. Here's how it works.
content:
Over time, washing and environmental damage leave swirl marks and fine scratches in your paint.

What is paint correction?

A multi-stage machine polishing process that removes imperfections from the clear coat, restoring depth and clarity.

The process

We assess the paint, choose the right pads and compounds, and carefully polish each panel to remove defects without damaging the finish.

The result

A glossy, reflective finish that looks better than new. Pair it with ceramic coating for lasting protection.
